penalties.description: Cannot determine task completion or timing from educational presentation frames.

consensus_score.reasoning: Claude identified this as an educational presentation about FLS programs rather than an actual task performance, while GPT-4o attempted to score what it perceived as a task execution. Given the fundamental disagreement about video content, Claude's assessment that no actual Peg Transfer task is visible takes precedence as it explains why no coherent task phases or reliable timing could be established.

claude_value.description: Cannot determine task completion or timing from educational presentation frames.

gpt_value.description: Two rings left the field of view and were not retrievable.

disagreements.rationale: Claude's assessment that this is an educational presentation rather than a task performance is more consistent with zero confidence and inability to detect coherent phases.
If no actual task performance is visible, completion time cannot be measured.
No phases can be detected if the video does not contain an actual task performance.
Incomplete task results in automatic zero score regardless of other factors.
